free_text: Excitation functions of some (n,p), (n,2n) and
(n,alpha) reactions on nickel, zirconium and niobium
isotopes in the energy range 13.63 - 14.83 MeV.
Final publication
Contains
details of experimental setup + some decay data.
High purity nickel, zirconium and niobium foils,
each sandwiched between two aluminium foils.
J-25 neutron generator
Used for determination
of neutron flux densities.
120 keV deuterons. Neutron energy versus angular
position of the sample with respect to the deuteron-
beam direction, was determined by measuring the ratio
of the Zr-89 to Nb-92-m specific activities produced
both in the Zr and Nb foils by (n,2n) reactions.
Flux densities were (0.8-4) x 10**8 n/cm2/s for J-25
Count rates at the end of irradiations were corrected
for dead time loss, coincidence effects, detector
efficiency and gamma transition intensities.
Total uncertainty includes statistical and
systematic components. The principal sources of
systematic uncertainty were:
Sample weight                          (0.5%)
Irradiation time                       (0.5%)
Irradiation geometry                   (3.%)
Error in neutron flux                  (5.%)
Determination of detector counts (1.-5.%)
Absorption of gamma rays in the sample  (1.%)
Detector efficiency                     (3.%)
Decay data                              (1.%)
Data are taken from Conf. on Nucl. Data for
Sci. and Technol.,Juelich 1991, p.355, Table 1,2 and 3.
HW
HW.-Institute code corrected.-
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Cosmetic changes, Common section and codes for
Err-analys were added. Code for facility was changed.
